concept_options.title: The Enzyme With Two Jobs
concept_options.hook: The fructose enzyme blamed for stress may also help liver cells survive it.
concept_options.visual_approach: Clean pathway animation: fructose/fat enters a hepatocyte, KHK lights up, the screen splits into adaptive IRE1α-XBP1 versus apoptotic PERK-CHOP outcomes.
concept_options.target_audience: Scientific colleagues, liver/metabolism researchers, and LinkedIn/X science followers
key_points: KHK rises during fructose-and-fat nutrient stress.
KHK aligns with adaptive IRE1α-XBP1 signaling across cell, mouse, and human data.
KHK knockdown shifts cells toward proteotoxic stress and apoptosis.
Therapeutic strategies may need selective enzymatic inhibition rather than complete loss.
concept_options.core_message: KHK is not just a fructose enzyme; it is also part of a liver-cell stress adaptation system.
concept_options.why_this_works: It leads with the paper’s surprising paradox, which is more memorable than a standard methods/results summary.

concept_options.title: A Liver Cell’s Stress Switch
concept_options.hook: When nutrients overload the ER, liver cells must choose: adapt or die.
concept_options.visual_approach: A hepatocyte as a tiny factory under overload: packages pile up in the ER, KHK helps route stress to adaptive folding support, and KHK loss tips the factory into alarm/apoptosis.
concept_options.target_audience: General science audience and students who need the pathway explained visually
key_points: Fructose plus saturated fat creates a nutrient-overload model.
The ER responds through UPR branches.
Without KHK, protein aggregates and apoptotic signals rise.
concept_options.core_message: The discovery is about cellular fate: KHK helps hepatocytes resolve stress instead of collapsing into apoptosis.
concept_options.cta: Share with colleagues studying MASLD, MASH, ER stress, or nutrient signaling.
concept_options.why_this_works: It turns abstract pathway acronyms into a relatable factory-stress story, making it accessible beyond specialists.

concept_options.title: Why Blocking Isn’t Deleting
concept_options.hook: A drug target can be useful—and dangerous to erase completely.
concept_options.visual_approach: Therapeutic decision board: “reduce fructose flux” versus “preserve ER protection,” ending on selective enzymatic inhibition as the nuanced takeaway.
concept_options.target_audience: Translational researchers, clinicians, drug-development audience, funders
key_points: KHK contributes to steatosis through fructose metabolism.
Complete KHK loss disrupts ER proteostasis.
The study argues for separating enzymatic activity from protective protein function.
concept_options.core_message: The therapeutic lesson is precision: inhibit the harmful activity without losing the adaptive stress-protection role.
concept_options.cta: Use this paper to rethink KHK-targeted approaches in MASLD/MASH.
concept_options.why_this_works: It foregrounds the clinical implication and makes the work feel relevant to therapy design rather than only cell biology.

selected_concept.rationale: Agent recommendation pending user approval: c1 is the most balanced quick video because it is surprising, accurate, and works for both scientific and professional social audiences.

stages.approach: Write a 60-second narration and on-screen caption script around the KHK paradox.
Plan 6-8 motion-graphic scenes using diagram cards, pathway arrows, and split-screen stress outcomes.

tools.why_this_provider: Available locally and well-suited for pathway diagrams.
No image-generation providers are configured, so the plan avoids paid/generated images.
No TTS provider is configured; user can record voiceover or approve caption-led delivery.

stages.approach: Build diagram-first assets from the supplied article text and DOI; use user-provided figures only if supplied/approved.
Assemble timing, captions, transitions, and optional music ducking plan.

tools.why_this_provider: Remotion is available and best fits data cards, captions, and pathway animation.
Not selected because HyperFrames is unavailable in this environment.

stages.approach: Render a 1080p vertical or square LinkedIn-ready video after runtime approval.

quality_tradeoffs.recommendation: Use your recorded voice for credibility if possible; otherwise proceed caption-led.
quality_tradeoffs.quality_impact: A real author voice increases trust and shareability, while caption-led is fastest.

quality_tradeoffs.tradeoff: Use article figures versus simplified diagrams
quality_tradeoffs.recommendation: Use simplified animated diagrams unless you provide figure image files.
quality_tradeoffs.quality_impact: Simplified diagrams are clearer on mobile; paper figures add authenticity but may be visually dense.

alternative_paths.description: Author-voice Remotion explainer with diagrams and captions
Caption-led Remotion explainer with optional music
Premium AI visuals/TTS path after configuring providers
alternative_paths.what_changes: Unlocks AI images and polished TTS; requires API keys.

voice_selection.rationale: No configured TTS provider is available; author-recorded voice is preferable for a paper announcement.

tts.explanation: Best credibility for a scientific paper announcement.
Fastest available fallback because TTS is unavailable.

music.explanation: Available music search; suitable for subtle background only.

metadata.preflight_status: degraded: Remotion available; HyperFrames, TTS, image generation, and video generation unavailable.

decisions.subject: Video production pipeline

options_considered.label: Animated explainer
options_considered.reason: Best fit for turning a scientific paper into diagrams, captions, and concise narration.

options_considered.label: Cinematic trailer
options_considered.reason: Would need configured video generation and is less precise for pathway science.

options_considered.label: Avatar spokesperson
options_considered.reason: Would require avatar/TTS tooling that is unavailable.

decisions.reason: Paper announcement needs accuracy and pathway clarity more than cinematic footage.

decisions.subject: Render runtime

options_considered.label: Remotion
options_considered.reason: Available and strong for React-based scientific scene cards, captions, and diagrams.

options_considered.label: HyperFrames
options_considered.reason: Could be strong for kinetic typography but is unavailable in this environment.
options_considered.rejected_because: runtime not available on this machine

options_considered.label: FFmpeg-only
options_considered.reason: Useful for simple stitching but weaker for custom motion-graphics pathway animation.

decisions.reason: Recommended pending user approval because it is available and fits the data-explainer grammar.

decisions.subject: Recommended concept

options_considered.label: The Enzyme With Two Jobs
options_considered.reason: Most balanced and surprising for a quick paper announcement.

options_considered.label: A Liver Cell’s Stress Switch
options_considered.reason: Most accessible for general audiences but slightly less focused on novelty.

options_considered.label: Why Blocking Isn’t Deleting
options_considered.reason: Best translational angle but narrower audience.

decisions.reason: Recommended pending user approval for widest high-quality impact.

findings.title: Runtime requires user approval
findings.description: Remotion is recommended because HyperFrames is unavailable, but the user still must approve render path before production.
findings.action: Ask user to approve Remotion or choose FFmpeg-only fallback.
